_metadata.description: Telesat Lightspeed constellation orbital parameters compiled from Wikipedia and public announcements.
sources: SpaceNews reporting on Telesat LEO constellation
notes: Originally announced as 120 satellites in 2016, expanded to ~300 in 2017, then 1,600+ in 2020.
Current configuration: 198 satellites (14 SpaceX Falcon 9 launches, up to 18 satellites each).
Manufacturer switched from Thales Alenia Space to MDA in August 2023 (saving ~$2 billion).
Phase 1 pathfinder test satellite launched 2018; LEO 3 demonstration satellite launched July 2023.
SpaceX contracted for 14 Lightspeed launches starting mid-2026.
Customers: Viasat, Space Norway, Orange, ADN Telecom.

shells.notes: Polar orbit shell for global coverage including polar regions. Inclination ~99 deg (sun-synchronous/near-polar).
Inclined orbit shell for mid-latitude coverage. Provides higher capacity over populated regions.

derived_orbital_parameters.description: Pre-computed orbital parameters for each shell

satellite_hardware.manufacturer: MDA (formerly contracted to Thales Alenia Space)
satellite_hardware.notes: Satellites equipped with optical ISL for mesh networking.

system_performance.notes: 16-24 Tb/s total system capacity; ~8 Tb/s available for customers.
